group.description: Optional file-level group used as a command prefix for all commands in this file.

runtimes.description: Runtime definitions keyed by alias (for example: deno, py).

include.description: List of additional local directories (relative to current directory) where Fire should load fire config files. Search is direct-level only (non-recursive).

x-arg-config.description: Reusable argument placeholder behavior, usually consumed via YAML anchors.

commands.description: Command definitions keyed by command name. The top-level command name `cli` is reserved and cannot be defined (validation error example: 'Property cli is not allowed.').

cli.description: Reserved command name. `commands.cli` is not allowed at the top level because `cli` is used internally by Fire.

prefix.description: Namespace prefix used in CLI paths.

sdk.description: Runtime SDK identifier used in runtime expressions.

runner.description: Default runner command used to execute runtime logic.

check.description: Health-check command to verify runtime availability.

fallback_runner.description: Fallback runner command used when `check` fails.

paths.description: Glob patterns where runtime scripts/functions are discovered.

placeholder.description: Placeholder template for positional args. Example: '{{n}}', '$n'.

on_unused_args.description: Behavior when extra args are passed and not consumed by placeholders.

stringOrStringArray.description: A single command string or a sequence of command strings.

commandEntry.description: Command shorthand string or full command object.

<<.description: YAML merge key used to reuse anchored blocks.

check.description: Pre-check command; can be paired with `fallback_runner`.

runner.description: Command runner prefix (for example to run inside a container shell).

before.description: Command executed before the main action.

description.description: Short user-facing description shown in listings and completion.

placeholder.description: Placeholder template for this command (same semantics as `x-arg-config.placeholder`).

on_unused_args.description: Behavior for unused args at this command level.

exec.description: Shell command(s) to execute. If an array is provided, commands run in order.

eval.description: Runtime expression(s) to evaluate (for example: py:func("{1}")).

compute.description: Computed variables map (name -> expression), evaluated before execution. If expression uses a known runtime prefix (<runtime>:<code>) it is evaluated in that runtime; otherwise it is executed as a shell command.

macros.description: Macro substitutions map used in `exec` and nested command strings.

commands.description: Nested subcommands keyed by subcommand name.
